observations.managementAction: Management promised to discuss with concerned officials to allocate budget for the said positions.
observations.reason: Creation of positions in this office was not given priority due to budget issues.

observations.recommendation: 32. Direct the Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention on the preparation and submission of the required bank reconciliation statements considering that the variance had already accumulated to ₱4.23 million and prepare subsidiary ledgers per bank accounts; and
observations.managementAction: Management promised to look into the work arrangements of the Accounting Office and will assign a personnel that will initiate the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant cannot start the preparation because of her workload.

observations.recommendation: 33. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to update religiously the cash books and the data reflected be preferably considered in the preparation of the bank reconciliation statements to ensure that any reconciling items will be accordingly adjusted.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to look into the work arrangements of the Accounting Office and will assign a personnel that will initiate the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant cannot start the preparation because of her workload.

observations.recommendation: 34. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to determine, once and for all, the collectability of these Receivable accounts together with the dormant cash advances and to document the request for write-off from the Commission on Audit of the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to determine the collectability of these accounts and initiate the request for write this CY 2020.
observations.reason: The request for write-off for the long outstanding receivable accounts were not given priority by Management.

observations.recommendation: 35. Require the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the proper process in recording and overall management of RPT and SET Receivables as prescribed under Section 248 of RA No. 7160 and Section 20, Volume I of  New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units;
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer promised to comply with the provision in Section 20 Volume I of NGAS for LGUs.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was not able to provide the Accounting Office the duly certified list of taxpayers and their amounts due for the year.

observations.recommendation: 36. Require the Municipal Accountant to credit accounts RPT and SET Receivables upon collection pursuant to Section 24 and 25, Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System;
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer promised to comply with the provision in Section 20 Volume I of NGAS for LGUs.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was not able to provide the Accounting Office the duly certified list of taxpayers and their amounts due for the year.

observations.recommendation: 37. Cause the posting of a Notice of Delinquency in the Payment of Real Property Tax at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall and in other publicly accessible place and conspicuous place in each barangay of the municipality in accordance with Section 254 of RA No. 7160; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer promised to comply with the provision in Section 20 Volume I of NGAS for LGUs.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was not able to provide the Accounting Office the duly certified list of taxpayers and their amounts due for the year.

observations.recommendation: 38. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger pertaining to RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer promised to comply with the provision in Section 20 Volume I of NGAS for LGUs.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was not able to provide the Accounting Office the duly certified list of taxpayers and their amounts due for the year.

observations.recommendation: 39. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strengthen accounting controls over liabilities by requiring the Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly the schedule of payables due to concerned agencies during the month and that remittances must be based on the actual payroll deductions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The review and validation of entries of the negative balances was not given priority by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: 40. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant carefully analyze transaction before recording in the books and to update entries of both records. Assign personnel to review the entries made to each subsidiary account with negative balances and effect the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The review and validation of entries of the negative balances was not given priority by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: 41. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation based on available documents especially for those recently acquired properties and make the necessary adjustment on the affected accounts. Henceforth, depreciation must be computed for all the properties of the Municipality periodically to ensure fair presentation of depreciation and related accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant again promised to comply with the recommendation and provide depreciation to the Agency’s PPEs.
observations.reason: Provision of depreciation was not given priority. The Municipal Accountant explained that the absence of the needed documents added to the difficulty in the provision of depreciation.

observations.recommendation: 42. Create a General Services Officer (GSO) position that will discharge on a full time basis the property custodianship functions and the receipt, issuance and recording of the procurement and issuance of supplies and property; maintain updated Property Cards per category on all property, plant and equipment as recorded in the list of Property, Plant and Equipment of accounting records; and facilitate the proper conduct of the physical count and preparation of the RPCPPE;
observations.managementAction: Management reviewed the composition of the Agency’s Inventory Committee and promised to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The conduct of inventory-taking of the Agency’s PPE was not given priority. The non-maintenance of property ledger cards added to the difficulty in the conduct of inventory-taking. The creation of GSO position was not also considered due to budget issues.

observations.recommendation: 43. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards consistent with their accounting records; and
observations.managementAction: Management reviewed the composition of the Agency’s Inventory Committee and promised to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Maintenance of property ledger cards in the Accounting Office was not prioritized.

observations.recommendation: 44. Maintain and renew all Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment every three years for the signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared RPCPPE or based on the old Memorandum Receipts on file.
observations.managementAction: Management reviewed the composition of the Agency’s Inventory Committee and promised to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Maintenance of ARE was not given priority.

observations.recommendation: 47. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Supply Officer of the Municipality to submit the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels and ensure that the driver’s trip tickets are properly accomplished and attached to the disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: Management directed the Municipal Accountant to prepare the said reports and submit them to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: The preparation of monthly reports of fuel consumption was not prioritized by Management.

observations.recommendation: 48. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned Municipal officials and employees to strictly comply with the mandatory submission of perfected contracts and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for a timely and complete conduct of auditorial and technical review, the results of which will be used in post-audit of disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to direct the BAC to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The submission of the copies of the perfected contracts were not given priority by the BAC.

observations.recommendation: 50. We further recommended that the said plan be properly implemented to ensure the efficient management of solid waste generated within its jurisdiction.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to finalize Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.reason: Management created Solid Waste Management Board (SWMB) but the plan is under revision.

observations.recommendation: 54. We also recommended the Sanguniang Bayan to devise an ordinance that will assist the Treasury Office in assessing the basis for CTC tax, such as requiring the CTC taxpayers to file a sworn declaration of total earnings derived from business, compensation, exercise of profession and income from real property during the preceding year.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason:
The provisions of the Municipal Tax Code was not fully implemented.

Provision of ordinance that will assist the Treasury in assessing the basis for CTC collection was not considered.

observations.recommendation: 55. That the Office of the Municipal Accountant give preferential attention on the preparation and submission of the required bank reconciliation statements pursuant to Section 474 of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 considering that the variance had already accumulated to ₱3,060,678.41; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to prepare the MBRS and submit them to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: The preparation of Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) was not given priority due to voluminous duties of the Municipal Accountant. The lack of personnel also added to the difficulty in preparing BRS.

observations.recommendation: 56. That the cash books of the Municipal Treasurer be updated religiously and the data reflected be preferably considered in the preparation of the bank reconciliation statements to ensure that any reconciling items will be accordingly adjusted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to prepare the MBRS and submit them to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: The preparation of Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) was not given priority due to voluminous duties of the Municipal Accountant. The lack of personnel also added to the difficulty in preparing BRS.

observations.recommendation: 60. Create a General Services Officer (GSO) position that will discharge on a full time basis the property custodianship functions and the receipt, issuance and recording of the procurement and issuance of supplies and property; maintain updated Property Cards per category on all property, plant and equipment as recorded in the list of Property, Plant and Equipment of accounting records; and facilitate the proper conduct of the physical count and preparation of the RPCPPE;
observations.managementAction: The Management will reconsider the establishment of General Services Office that will discharge in a full time basis the property custodianship, receipt, issuance and recording the agency’s assets.
observations.reason: The established Inventory Committee was non-operating in 2018. No consideration to establish GSO position due to budget issues.

observations.recommendation: 61. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards consistent with their accounting records;
observations.managementAction: The Management will reconsider the establishment of General Services Office that will discharge in a full time basis the property custodianship, receipt, issuance and recording the agency’s assets.
observations.reason: Maintenance of property ledger cards in the Accounting Office was not prioritized.

observations.recommendation: 62. Require both offices to reconcile and work out on tracing any resulting difference, establish its existence/non-existence and determine from there any further action that may be undertaken; and
observations.managementAction: The Management will reconsider the establishment of General Services Office that will discharge in a full time basis the property custodianship, receipt, issuance and recording the agency’s assets.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of difference was not given priority by Management.

observations.recommendation: 63. Maintain and renew all Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment every three years for the signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared RPCPPE or based on the old Memorandum Receipts on file.
observations.managementAction: The Management will reconsider the establishment of General Services Office that will discharge in a full time basis the property custodianship, receipt, issuance and recording the agency’s assets.
observations.reason: Maintenance of the ARE was not given priority.

observations.recommendation: 64. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute the depreciation based on available documents especially for those recently acquired properties and make the necessary adjustment on the affected accounts. Henceforth, depreciation must be computed for all the properties of the Municipality periodically to ensure fair presentation of depreciation and related accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will compute the depreciation of the agency’s assets and effect them in the financial statements of the ensuing year.
observations.reason: The provision of depreciation of the agency’s assets was not prioritize by the Accountant.

observations.recommendation: 65. We recommended to require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to provide the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year a list of all on-going and to be implemented PPAs during the year. Further, a monthly monitoring report on the status of the projects must be submitted. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer must see to it that verification of the projects’ status as reported in its monthly report in accordance with the frequency of verification provided in Item 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 be timely requested and the necessary documents be submitted to facilitate the inspection by COA-TAS.
observations.managementAction: The BAC will submit the list of on-going PPAs to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Preparation and submission of the list of all on-going PPAs during the year was not given attention by the BAC.

observations.recommendation: 66. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the mandatory submission of perfected contracts and their supporting documents to COA for a timely and complete conduct of auditorial and technical review, the results of which will be used in post-audit of disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The Management again committed to furnish the Auditor copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders.
observations.reason: Due to lack of coordination between the BAC Chairman, the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management be more prudent and frugal in the procurement of medical equipment and supplies especially during this trying time. Henceforth, require the end –users and the BAC to abide and be updated with rules and regulations on procurement all the times.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant check the propriety and validity of supporting documents before certifying that all supporting documents are complete.  Otherwise, the same is subject to audit suspension or disallowance.

We recommend the management to expedite all means to release to the qualified beneficiaries the financial assistance and submit the liquidation to COA for review and evaluation.  Henceforth, management is reminded to be always on the defensive end in handling our limited government funds and be mindful of meeting the deadlines set by these said guidelines.
We recommend that management require immediate full liquidation of the subject cash advance, otherwise, withhold the accountable officer’s salary until the cash advance is fully liquidated.

We recommend that management submit the necessary supporting documents to establish the propriety of the distribution of the relief goods, medicines and medical and laboratory supplies such as inventory of the supplies at year-end and distribution list signed by the recipients.  Henceforth, require the accounting department to follow the relevant rules and regulations in recording the procurement of supplies for distribution.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer timely submits the Reports of Collection and Deposit to the Municipal Accountant’s Office, and for the former to record collection at period revenue were collected in compliance with the PAG 1 so not to misstate the yearend financial statements.

We recommend that management designate personnel to man the sorting, segregation, composting and recycling of waste to reduce the environmental risk and health hazards these materials have to the constituent.

We recommend that management require that the Trip Tickets be serially numbered for the purpose of monitoring and controlling the use of government vehicles and consumption of fuel. Moreover, require the Municipal Drivers to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels for each vehicle certified by the driver and approved by the Administrative Officer for verification by the Auditor. Also, a Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and submitted on a monthly basis by the Agency personnel concerned in compliance with COA Circular No. 77-61 to determine the reasonableness of the Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expenses.

We recommend that management require the concerned officials/employees to notify the Accounting Office whether travels were completed or withdrawn and submit the required supporting documents enumerated in Item 1.2.4.1 of COA Circular 2012-001.  Otherwise, the amount of ₱69,618.64 is subject to audit suspension or disallowance.  Henceforth, stop paying reimbursements for traveling expenses unless the travel is completed evidenced with the travel and official business documents.  To cover expenses from upcoming travel, the management may grant cash advances instead, to be liquidated within one calendar month from the employee’s return to permanent official station pursuant to Section 2.0 of COA Circular No. 96-004.

We recommend that henceforth, management  create a well-planned detailed engineering plan and adequately review and evaluate program of works in all projects to ensure completion of programmed projects as scheduled.

We recommend that the Municipal Vice Mayor make a representation to the VMLP Bohol Chapter to have the amount paid as annual dues to the league be reviewed; taking into consideration the needs of each municipality, the pandemic that may have cancelled the activities planned for CY 2020, thus, expected to have left material unexpended fund balance of the VMLP; and suggest that it be lessened to pave way for other necessary expenditures of the respective agency.

We recommend that management follow and be updated always of the rules and regulations concerning procurement.  Henceforth, management should always manage the exposure of government funds to any possible risk by following strictly the controls placed already by existing laws.

We recommend the BAC to indicate in the abstract of quotation any deficiencies which lead to the disqualification of a bidder.  Henceforth, management is advised to abide by the provisions of Section 8.b.vi of the Revised IRR of the RA 9184.
We recommend that Municipal Accountant always check not just the presence but the content as well of supporting documents to ascertain that all charges are correct and complete.

We shall appreciate submission of the financial statements as soon as possible in compliance of the aforecited circular. We further recommend that management facilitate for the adoption of the Electronic New Government Accounting System to help the Municipal Accounting Office speed up the process of the FS preparation.

We recommended that Management:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Office of the
Auditor all paid DVs for CY 2020 still in their possession, ensure submission
of paid DVs on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month, and avoid
voluminous submissions of DVs pertaining to transactions for several months
all at once;
b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office all the
DVs, official receipts including the corresponding reports of each month on
or before the fifth day of the ensuing month; and
c) Remind the concerned officials and employees that continuous non-
compliance and non-submission of DVs may warrant issuance of NS or ND
by the Auditor to persons liable or responsible, as stated under Section 8.4 of
COA Circular 2009-006.

We recommended that Management:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to use available data found in the annual
reports of Government PPAs for prior years starting 2014, and closely
monitor the CIP account through maintenance of subsidiary records and
regular coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to identify all completed
projects for proper reclassification and recording to their appropriate asset
account;
b) Require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to maintain ledger cards
consistent with Stock Cards, Property Cards, SLC and PPELC, and conduct
periodic reconciliation of property and accounting records to come up with
accurate reports;
c) Require the concerned personnel to conduct complete annual physical count
of Inventories and PPE, reconcile the Property and Accounting records after
the physical count and submit to the Auditor the RPCI and RPCPPE for
verification in accordance with Section 124 of Manual on the NGAS for
LGUs, Volume 1; and
d) Ensure that ending balances of PPE are carried over properly to the next
period to prevent discrepancies between the ending balance and their
respective beginning balances of the ensuing year.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to compute the depreciation
expense of the PPE with available documents, using the method prescribed
in the aforementioned PAGs; and
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the provision of
depreciation for the assets procured/constructed from year 2015 onwards,
review PPE schedules, and gather supporting documents attached on the
affected accounts. Henceforth, depreciation must be computed for all the
depreciable properties of the Municipality periodically to ensure fair
presentation of depreciation and related accounts in the financial
statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to prepare SLs per bank accounts and give
preferential attention on the preparation and submission of the BRSs
pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011; and
b) Municipal Treasurer to regularly update the cash books and reflect the
reconciling items per BRS, and close accounts that have become dormant and
revert back to the Source Agency or the Bureau of the Treasury, as the case
maybe.

We recommended that the Accounting Office:
a) Prepare and maintain SLs as many as necessary for all the accounts
appearing in the financial statements to determine the correctness of account
balances at any given period; and
b) Use the available data in the accounting records as beginning balances of the
SLs to facilitate preparation and maintenance thereof.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management facilitate immediately the
request for write-off of the aforementioned account balances so that the same will be
derecognized in the financial statements, subject to the procedures and requirements
set forth in COA Circular 2016-005.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management require:
a) The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the
prescribed rules on the recognition and setting-up of RPT and SET
Receivables under Section 248 of RA No. 7160 and Section 20, Volume I of
NGAS for LGUs;
b) The Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort in improving the collection
efficiency of the Municipality by posting the Notice of Delinquency in the
Payment of RPT at the main entrance of Municipal Hall and other publicly
accessible place and conspicuous place in each barangay as required under
Section 254 of RA No. 7160 or mailing demand letters to its constituents
individually in order to compel them to settle their tax liability; and
Management Comment:
The Accounting Office promised to study the prescribed rules on proper recording of
RPT and SET Receivables. Further, in order to improve the RPT collection efficiency rate
of the Municipality, Management will require the concerned offices to post Notice of
Delinquency and/or demand letters for each delinquent tax payers for the payment of RPT
and SET.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
reclassify the current portion of the long-term loan payable amounting to ₱0.82
million to its proper classification in the Statement of Financial Position and Notes to
Financial Statements in order to provide a more accurate view of the Agency’s ability
to pay current liabilities as they fall due.

We recommended that Management require:
a) The Municipal Treasurer to maintain a sufficient amount of checks that
would cover current and future disbursements to avoid issuance of MC; and
b) The Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor
all DVs and their supporting documents still in their possession, and ensure
submission of paid DVs on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month.

We recommended that Management:
a) Strengthen accounting controls over the remittance of Inter-Agency Payables
through strict coordination between the personnel in-charge of payroll,
recording of related liabilities and payment to ensure that remittances are
based on actual collections and payroll deductions;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the correctness of
GL postings to the Inter-Agency payable accounts through reconciliation of
data between JEVs and GL and proper supervision of posting to GL;
c) Require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere and observe deadlines for
remittances to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and Philhealth in order to eliminate
surcharges/interest; and
d) Communicate and reconcile with concerned agencies the correct amount of
withholding per employee.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a detailed review of
the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled accounts. Thereafter, make
the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct
balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in recording and
classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of
reciprocal accounts are at all times the same.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
a) Strictly comply with the rules and regulations on fuel consumption to
minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures through usage of
RIV or its equivalent, and properly accomplished driver’s trip ticket in order
to ensure that government vehicles are used reasonably and to avoid audit
suspension or disallowance thereof; and
b) Submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels within
10 days of the succeeding month for validation.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management require the concerned
official to submit copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders and their complete
supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five days after perfection for
purposes of review thereof and technical evaluation by COA TAS.

We recommended that Management require the:
a) BAC Technical Working Group and other officials concerned to submit
immediately the required documents for audit;
b) BAC Chairman to strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 3720 as
amended, Executive Order No. 49 and other pertinent laws in the
procurement of drugs, medicines and laboratory supplies; and
c) Municipal Accountant and her staff to conduct thorough review and ensure
completeness of supporting documents before effecting any payments
particularly on the purchase of drugs and medicines in compliance with COA
Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of
monthly trial balance and quarterly financial statements within the
reglementary period; and
b) Require the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere to the deadline of
Status of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations, as required in Section
70 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUS.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct Officers and Employees with cash advances to immediately submit
their liquidation papers, otherwise, require the immediate withholding of
salary of those officers and employees who were unable to comply with the
requirement;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the liquidation of
outstanding cash advances and send written demand on the submission of
liquidation report within 10 days before the 30-day period, pursuant to
Section 5.l of COA Circular No. 97-002;
c. Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those who have
unliquidated cash advances unless the previous ones are fully settled; and
d. Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring AOs and be
reminded that the Auditor may order the suspension of their salaries, as cited
in Section 9.2 and 9.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-004.

We recommended that Management require the:
a) Municipal Accountant to make the adjusting entries in the books of
accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts and to
exercise care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial
transactions; and
b) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Engineer to furnish the Municipal
Accountant the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA), Trust Agreement
and/or other documents that would indicate the existence of mutual
agreement for projects that require transfer of funds between the Source
Agency/NGA and Implementing Agency/the Municipality.

In order to prepare an optimal and timely SEF Budget, we recommended that
Management and the LSB:
a. Ensure that the prescribed planning and budgeting tools are effectively utilized;
and
b. Strictly conform to the timelines in the planning and budgeting process as
prescribed under DepEd, DBM & DILG Joint Circular No. 2017-01.
We also recommended that Management, in coordination with LSB, ensure that
the total share on the additional one percent tax on real property from previous year
be completely appropriated in succeeding year’s SEF Annual Budget to ensure funding
of priority programs, projects and activities of public schools in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Require the Budget/Accounting Office to prepare and submit to the
NDRRMC, through the OCD, and to the Office of the Auditor the report on
the receipt and utilization of future cash donations;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary correcting entry to
adjust the balances of the affected accounts, and promptly monitor the
transactions in the TF books most especially fund transfers and diligently
apply relevant accounting treatment; and
c) Immediately issue demand letter for the liquidation of cash advance to the
former Municipal Treasurer in order to assess the validity and propriety of
the utilization of the donation.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the
LDRRM Officer to secure immediately the needed documents to facilitate the timely
preparation of the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and
submit a copy thereof to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the
end of each month, to facilitate verification thereof.
Likewise, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile the differences between the
balance of previous years’ unutilized appropriation per LDRRMF Utilization Report
and Notes to FS.

We recommended that Management:
a) Be reminded of provisions in COA Circular No. 2012-001 which
prevents/disallow the grant of CA to elected officials for non-traveling related
purposes in order to maintain efficiency and effectiveness of usage of funds; and
b) Procure necessary items on a timely manner during state of emergency.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC to require the
aforementioned suppliers to prepare or submit the Mayor’s/Business Permit, OSS and
the Income/Business Tax Returns. After these documents were verified and examined,
the same must be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for the required validation to
avoid audit suspension of the involved transactions.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile the balances of the Cashbook and General Ledgers.  Identify the items that need adjustment and record them appropriately in the affected books.  Likewise, require the accountable officers to reconcile their cashbook balances with the accounting records at least quarterly pursuant to the Handbook of cash examinations, so that adjustments, if any can be promptly taken up in the affected books and correctness of the balances assured.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant provided reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for transfers of LGU’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund account by year of transfer and to disclose in the year-end Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balances of the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Management provided subsidiary ledger.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to require the Municipal Accountant to present appropriate schedules showing the details and composition of the accounts Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-ibig and PhilHealth to support the balances of these accounts reported in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Management provided the schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the LDRRMC to monitor and evaluate the use and disbursements of the Calamity Fund and to deliberate and discuss problems encountered by the LGU during actual disasters/calamities during the past year/s to be included the LDRRM Plan of the ensuing year.  Henceforth, maximize utilization of the pre disaster preparedness programs and/or activities so that the intended purpose is attained/achieved as provided in Rule 18 of RA 10121.
observations.managementAction: Management has tried to maximize the utilization.
observations.reason: From the total appropriation of ₱2,521,714.72, ₱2,061,665.79 was obligated to respond to covid.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that initially, appropriations for the establishment of MRFs be included in the SWM budget.  Consequently, MRFs be established in every barangay or cluster of barangays in the Municipality.  The LGU may pilot a single barangay to establish MRF project in order to institute a system of knowledge in establishing one as well as to gain commitment and participation of barangay officials on the proper administration and operation of MRFs.
observations.managementAction: The management has constructed MRF and bought recycling equipment.
observations.reason: The MRF is not yet operational as there is no personnel to monitor and control the activities in the MRF as discussed in Finding No. 1 of Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to ask a formal approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) on the use of above bank accounts based on the provisions in DOF Circular 001-2005 and transfer immediately any amount in excess of the authorized cash balance to any GFIs in Section 5.2.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant – Designate to compute the depreciation expense of the PPE with available documents, using the method prescribed in the aforementioned PAGs. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prioritize the provision of depreciation for the assets procured/constructed from year 2015 onwards, review PPE schedules, gather supporting documents attached on the disbursement vouchers related to the PPE and make necessary adjustments on the affected accounts. Henceforth, depreciation must be computed for all the properties of the Municipality periodically to ensure fair presentation of depreciation and related accounts in the financial statements.

Requiring the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to follow the proper process in recording RPT and SET Receivables pursuant to the provisions prescribed under Section 248 of RA No. 7160 and Section 20, Volume I of NGAS for LGUs;
Posting of Notice of Delinquency in the payment of RPT at the Municipal Hall and in other public/conspicuous places all over the Municipality, in accordance with Section 254 of RA No. 7160; and
Requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers pertaining to RPT and SET Receivables.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant, the LDRRM Officer, Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to review all available documents relative to LDRRMF and effect necessary adjustments to correct the amount presented in the financial statements and other documents;
Instruct the aforementioned officers to revisit and strictly follow the provisions of RA No. 10121, COA Circular No. 2012-002 and National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC) – Department of Budget Management (DBM) – Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for transfers of Agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer, receipts of NDRRMF by transferring agency, DRRMF from other LGUs by LGU and other sources by donor.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to strengthen accounting controls over liabilities by reviewing thoroughly the schedule of payables due to concerned agencies during each month and ensure that remittances are based on the actual payroll deductions. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant carefully analyze every transactions before recording them in the books and assign a personnel to review the entries made to each subsidiary account with negative balances and effect the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require all Accountable Officers to submit necessary documents to support their claims to the Office of the Municipal Accountant and direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents from January to December 2020 to the Office of the Auditor;
Evaluate the process of preparing the disbursement vouchers and necessary documents to prevent further occurrence of this deficiency in the succeeding years and be reminded that processing of vouchers must only be undertaken when they were properly approved and with complete documentation; and
Remind the concerned officials and employees of the administrative or criminal liability, under Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445, Section 23 (1) (3) of the Administrative Code of 1987 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code, which may be imposed as a consequence of their inability or refusal to render accounts without justifiable cause.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned Municipal officials and employees to strictly comply with the mandatory submission of copies of perfected contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five days after perfection for a timely and complete conduct of auditorial and technical review.

We also directed the attention of Management to Section 4.0, the Penalty Clause, of COA Circular No. 2009-001, which states that the inability of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements of the same must be subject to administrative disciplinary action and the transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents be may be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the preparation and approval of Driver’s Trip Tickets, in accordance with the provision cited in COA Circular No. 77-61, before every use of the Municipality’s vehicles and heavy equipment; and
Assign a personnel/supply officer for the preparation and submission of the required Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Summary of Requisition and Issue Vouchers to the Office of the Auditor for verification and audit purposes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMO to secure immediately the needed documents to facilitate the timely preparation of the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit a copy thereof to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, pursuant to the procedures of the aforecited Circular, to facilitate verification thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMO to prioritize the procurement of early warning systems, search rescue and retrieval equipment and other equipage useful for the disaster preparedness and disaster response rather than administrative purpose and assess the adequacy and availability of basic rescue and response equipment in the LDRRM Office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD focal person to strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations prescribing the guidelines in the formulation of Annual GPB, development of GAD activities and implementation and monitoring of the GPB; and utilize the GAD Fund only for acceptable PPAs that would address the identified gender issues in the locality based on RA 9710 or the Magna Carta for Women, PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and other relevant international commitments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LCPC to:
Actively perform their duties and responsibilities to ensure full strengthening and implementation of the LCPC PPAs; and
Widen the scope of the PPAs for LCPC as enumerated in Section II of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 and prioritize the PPAs with long term effects/involvement on children.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasury Office to implement collection strategies to further improve revenue assessment and collection.  Periodic collection performance and monitoring must be conducted to ensure efficient collection of projected income;
Instruct Municipal Treasurer or her authorized representative to examine the books, accounts, and other pertinent records of any person, partnership, corporation, or association subject to local taxes, fees and charges in order to ascertain, assess, and collect the correct amount of the tax, fee, or charge; and
Direct the Local Finance Committee or the Municipal Treasurer, Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Officer to formulate realistic income projections based on Statistics of past years’ experience and profile of local taxpayers and devise effective plan to avoid, mitigate and control the risks of possible non-collection of projected incomes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMO and Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) to initiate the creation and filling up of the mandatory positions prescribed under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC 2014-1 to properly institutionalize the organization of the LDRRMO and to have continuity in service and implementation of its programs.

We recommended that Management prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the LGU for the optimal benefit of the constituents. Further, personnel movement has to be effected in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing the entry of new applicants for lower level positions. We also recommended that Management initiate the appropriation of salaries for the two MGDH, specifically, the Municipal Administrator and the Municipal Engineer, and fill them accordingly to properly perform the functions of the offices concerned.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the SWM Focal Person to prioritize the approval of the MSWMP that will ensure the efficient management of solid wastes generated within the Municipality’s jurisdiction; and organize the SWMB, compliant with prescribed composition enumerated in Section 16 of RA No. 9003, that can greatly contribute to the promotion and implementation of measures for an effective solid waste management programs.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit explanation on the delayed submission of records and reports.  Henceforth, require them to cooperate and coordinate in the timely submission of require reports.  Otherwise, sanctions provided in Section 122 of P. D. 1445 or the State Audit Code of the Philippines could be imposed which is the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission on report submission.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to record income when collected and record remittance when deposited to properly establish cut-offs and accurately report cash and income accounts on a daily, monthly and yearly basis.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile the balances of the Cashbook and General Ledgers.  Identify the items that need adjustment and record them appropriately in the affected books.  Likewise, require the accountable officers to reconcile their cashbook balances with the accounting records at least quarterly pursuant to the Handbook of cash examinations, so that adjustments, if any can be promptly taken up in the affected books and correctness of the balances assured.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to continuously retrieve for documents to support the adjustment to be done to take-up these reconciling items in the books to arrive at a corrected balance.  A Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) shall be prepared for all reconciling items to effect adjustment thereof.  Please furnish this office copy of the JEV to correct/reconcile the balances.

We recommend that management avail the procedures to write off these receivables and instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the documents required per Item 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 in order to facilitate submission of request for write-off of dormant accounts which are no longer recoverable from deceased LGU official.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year so that the RPT and SET Receivables can be adjusted by the Municipal Accountant to reflect the correct balances at year-end.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to demand the concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers of their immediate submission of liquidation reports in order to properly and timely assess and monitor the project implementation of barangays and fairly present the assets of the municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reclassify as non-current assets, the portion of the receivables which do not conform to the criteria provided for in paragraph 76 of the IPSAS in order to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.

We recommend that management review the validity vis-à-vis the possibility of claim of the outstanding payables and cause the reversion of those without valid claim to the unappropriated surplus. The management is also reminded to be stringent in assessing expenses to be obligated at year end.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the accounting entries pertaining to these accounts to ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances and to effect the necessary adjustments.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for transfers of LGU’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund account by year of transfer and to disclose in the year-end Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balances of the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the long outstanding balance of ₱6,287,504.28, that also includes ₱2,887,337.32 errors and misclassifications as well as accounting entries pertaining to the ₱36,687.19 accounts to ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances and to effect the necessary adjustments in order to present a reliable balance of the Due to LGUs account.

We recommend that management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the accuracy of the amounts to be remitted to the barangays.
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the required Abstract of Real Property Collections in order to monitor and control the release of shares of Real Property and Community Taxes due the barangays.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to release and or remit in full the amount of ₱1,094,297.45 due the different barangays so that the same could be utilized in implementing the development projects of their respective areas.
The Municipal Treasure should strictly adhere to the prescribed period in releasing/remitting the amount representing the share in real property and community taxes of the barangays pursuant to Section 164 and 271 of the Local Government Code.

We recommend that management abide by the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 through the immediate utilization of the funds received, timely and proper liquidation of all implemented projects and remittance of the remaining balances thereof to source agency of the excess funds.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the accounting entries pertaining to these accounts to ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances and to effect the necessary adjustments.

It is recommended that with the assistance of the MPDC, projects identified have to be implemented within the year these were appropriated, to deliver the intended benefits to the constituents and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices in materials and feasibility of the projects in order to address the needs of the Municipality.  Further, conduct actual studies for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign funds to other projects attune with the present needs and constraints.

We recommend that management exhaust all available remedies provided by law and regulations in enforcing the immediate settlement of the audit disallowances in accordance with Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
We recommend that management keep up the good work and employ continuous efforts to improve the financial performance of its market, waterworks system and the LGU as a whole.

We recommend management to stop immediately the imposition of the unauthorized increased market rental fees and penalties and abide by the rates provided in the Market Code of the Municipality.  Require the Municipal Treasurer’s and Municipal Accountant’s Offices to treat as advance payments the excess rental fees and charges received from tenants and correspondingly compute discounts on the said overpayments.  Otherwise, an appropriate ordinance updating the Market Code of the Municipality has to be enacted before imposing the increased market rental fees and penalties.  Henceforth, management is called to conform to Section 132 of the Local Government Code in the imposition of its revenues and taxes.

We recommend management to require the Municipal Accountant to present appropriate schedules showing the details and composition of the accounts Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-ibig and PhilHealth to support the balances of these accounts reported in the financial statements.

Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate measures to control the charges made under the appropriations of programs, projects and activities, for Gender and Development, to which it was created; and
Institute strong internal control over municipal transactions to ensure that the municipal government has a balanced budget and that funds allocated for the delivery of basic services and development projects are utilized as intended.

We recommended that Management:
Judiciously plan the programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the 20 per cent EDF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 2, 2017; and
Promptly implement all the programs/projects/activities as embodied in the approved Annual Investment Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to the people.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasury Office to implement collection strategies to further improve revenue assessment and collection. We also recommended the Sanguniang Bayan to devise an ordinance that will assist the Treasury Office in assessing the basis for CTC tax, such as requiring the CTC taxpayers to file a sworn declaration of total earnings derived from business, compensation, exercise of profession and income from real property during the preceding year.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to take-up immediately these reconciling items in the books to arrive at a corrected balance.  A journal voucher shall be prepared for all reconciling items to effect adjustment thereof.

We recommend that management produce the disbursement vouchers of the above unrecorded checks totaling ₱1,408,518.68 otherwise, they will be subject for disallowance. Prospectively, checks should only be issued upon approval of the disbursement vouchers.

cash advances  for travel under the Receivables- Due from Officers and Employees account;
special purpose/time-bound undertakings  recorded to the appropriate account, Advances to Officers and Employees account
Require the Municipal Accountant to support all Receivable accounts with a duly certified aging schedule and retrieve all pertinent documents relative thereto to support the billing/collection letters and other appropriate action to enforce payment thereof, pursuant to Section 60 and 64 of the GAAM Volume I.

Otherwise, provide allowance for impairment and/or request write-off of the balance after exhausting all possible means to substantiate the account.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for real accounts such as the Due to NGAS account and provide an aging schedule.  Moreover, abide by the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-103 through the immediate utilization of the funds received, timely and proper liquidation of all implemented projects and remittance of the remaining balances thereof to source agency or seeking authority for the utilization of the excess funds.

We shall appreciate submission of the above source documents and reports as soon as possible.  If the observation is left uncorrected and submission of financial reports is delayed, its significance and usefulness to the users of the data is lost.  More importantly, it denies management the availability of reliable information on the financial condition and results of operations and cash flows of the municipality at year-end and at interim periods.

It is recommended that with the assistance of the MPDC, projects identified have to be implemented within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the constituents and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices in materials and feasibility of the projects in order to address the needs of the Municipality.  Further, conduct actual studies for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign funds to other projects attune with the present needs and constraints.

We recommend that management submit the complete set of approved plans and drawings including the detailed breakdown of the approved budget for the contract, unit cost analysis, program of work and the detailed engineering design for the project.

We further recommend that management practice the timely submission of complete set of contract documents to facilitate the review on the reasonableness of contract cost and duration.

We recommend management to submit copies of Acceptance Inspection Reports on procured items to the Auditor’s Office within 24 hours form acceptance of the items delivered to comply with the provision of Paragraph 6.09 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and for the Auditor to conduct timely inspection of supplies and materials delivered.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant observe proper account classification in the recording of released financial assistance to different implementing agencies and to adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013.
We recommend the management to strictly impose the withholding of salary on employees with cash advances which are not liquidated within the prescribed period.

We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) at the start of the year and the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommend that management require the LDRRMC to monitor and evaluate the use and disbursements of the Calamity Fund and to deliberate and discuss problems encountered by the LGU during actual disasters/calamities during the past year/s to be included the LDRRM Plan of the ensuing year.  Henceforth, maximize utilization of the pre disaster preparedness programs and/or activities so that the intended purpose is attained/achieved as provided in Rule 18 of RA 10121.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct an information campaign to all real property owners within the Municipality with emphasis of granting discounts when paid before due dates and the possibility of losing their property for not paying at all.

We recommend that management submit justification for failure to rescind the contract despite delay in the implementation of the project, non-adherence to Section 68 of the 2009 R-IRR of R.A. 9184 and explanation for other deficiencies noted.

We recommend that management ensure that the GAD funds are fully utilized and maximized in accordance with the guidelines set by DBM, NEDA and NCRFW to ensure that the objective of the fund may be attained.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to strengthen the control over the disbursements of the agency by strictly conforming to the documentary requirements enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and pursuant to Section 4 of PD 1145, before disbursing government funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a) Stop the practice of paying procured goods and services on reimbursement basis. Acquisition of goods and services is recommended to pass through the regular mode of procurement pursuant to R.A. 9184; and

b) Strictly adhere to the requirement that all payments must be made by check in the name of the supplier/contractor/payee pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that management strengthen accounting controls over liabilities by requiring the Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly the schedule of payables due to concerned agencies every month and remittances must be based on the actual collection vis-à-vis payroll deductions. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant carefully analyse transaction before recording in the books and update entries of both records. Further, assign personnel to review the entries made to each subsidiary account with negative balances and effect the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMFIP be prepared, reviewed and updated annually in accordance with the provisions indicated in COA Circular No. 2012-002 to monitor the activities to be funded by the current year disaster QRF and mitigation fund as well as the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF of previous years.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and instruct the LDRRM Officer to submit a copy thereof to the office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, pursuant to the procedures of the forecited Circular, to facilitate verification thereof.

We recommended that Management require the Office of the Municipal Accountant to fast track the submission of the said accounts complete with their respective supporting documents to facilitate timely review and verification of the LGUs CY 2017 financial transactions. This is without prejudice to our further audit action regarding the suspensions/disallowances that may be issued on the subject disbursements. It is also suggested that they evaluate their current system and existing manpower for any necessary improvement or augmentation to prevent the further occurrence of this deficiency in the succeeding financial years.

We also reminded the Management and the concerned officials and employees of the administrative or criminal liability, under Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445, Section 23 (1) (3) of the Administrative Code of 1987 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code, which may be imposed as a consequence of their inability or refusal to render accounts without justifiable cause.

We recommended that the Municipality enact a Municipal Ordinance establishing the Solid Waste Management Board, prepare Solid Waste Management Plan that will ensure the long term management of solid waste, implement segregation of waste at a household level, and stop the practice of open burning of garbage.
observations.managementAction: The Management will follow-up Environment Management Bureau.
observations.reason: The Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) of the Municipality was still under review of the Environment Management Bureau and not yet approved by the National Solid Waste Council (NSWC).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the concerned personnel to strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations prescribing the guidelines in the formulation of Annual GAD Plan and Budget, development of agency GAD activities and implementation and monitoring of the GAD Plan and Budget. Conduct GAD programs, projects and activities that are acceptable and would address the identified gender issues in the locality based on the Magna Carta for Women RA 9710 and its IRR, PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and other relevant international commitments.

We recommended that the Municipality establish the Local Council for the Protection of Children and separately provide the required allocation of 1 per cent if the IRA to be used for the strengthening and implementation of allowable programs, projects and activities of the LCPC, which are consistent with the approved annual Work and Financial Plan, Local Development Plan for Children, and Local Investment Plan and likewise, to ensure proper coordination and assistance in the adoption and implementation of the comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Accounting Office all the disbursement vouchers, official receipts including the corresponding reports of each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month; and the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the timetable on the submission of accounts as required under existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the agency create a GAD Focal Point System for better ways of performing its task. The above mentioned guidelines serve as the proper way of structuring the GAD Focal Point System. More projects and activities could be implemented, only if the GAD Focal Point System is fully-functional. Also, GFPS shall submit the agency’s   GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) within the month of January to February of each year to see to it that the GPB of the municipality is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB shall be forwarded to the DILG Regional Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. Actual GAD projects and activities should also be aligned with the GAD plan per COA Circular 14-001 and Joint Circular Memorandum 2013-01.

We recommended that the LDRRMF plan be prepared, reviewed and updated annually in accordance with the provisions indicated in COA Circular No. 2012-002 to monitor the activities to be funded by the current year disaster mitigation fund as well as the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF of previous years.

We recommended requiring the Local Development Council (LDC) to Submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan and Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the formats prescribed in Annex A and B of COA Circular No. 2012-002. We also recommended the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of the Quick Response Fund to the Special Trust Fund to ensure that the unspent balance are readily available for relief and recovery projects and activities within the next five (5) years.

We recommended that management strictly require each Department Head to prepare and submit to the General Services Office their respective PPMPs in order that a reviewed and consolidated APP is formulated by the BAC in accordance with Section 7, Rule II of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

Create a task force to identify the lots owned by the LGU and review if the same could be titled in the name of the LGU; Require the task force to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots. Require the Municipal Accountant to update/adjust the Land (201) account balance in the books of accounts based on the results of the identification of municipal-owned lots and its titling.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit exact amount of what has been withheld by the LGU to agencies concerned. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to investigate and identify these negative balances. If it can be substantiated with complete documents, appropriate adjustment should be effected to correct these deficiencies.

We therefore recommended that the annual inventory-taking of all assets or PPEs of the LGU be conducted each year pursuant to Section 124, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs, submit the RPCPPE and furnish a copy to the Auditor and reconcile the RPCPPE with the records of the Accountant and the GSO.

The same will be used as basis to ascertain and validate the balances recorded in the Financial Statements for CY 2015.
